title: A Fistful of Paintballs (1)

jokes.text: - I see you, chubby ass! - Oh! - I ain't chasing, chubby. - No, no, no! - Chubby-chaser. - No! You can't outrun me. I see you, fat-ass!
jokes.setup: Paintball chase scene with aggressive pursuit
jokes.punchline: Relentless fat-shaming while chasing someone who clearly can't escape
jokes.explanation: Low-hanging fruit relying on body-shaming for cheap laughs, but the relentless repetition has some comedic escalation

jokes.text: There's only one rule, Fat Neil. The prize goes to the last man standing. So to speak.
jokes.setup: Explaining paintball rules to someone called Fat Neil
jokes.punchline: The ironic 'so to speak' acknowledging the weight-based nickname
jokes.explanation: Clean wordplay with good economy - the 'so to speak' is a nice self-aware touch

jokes.text: If you were a dude, I'd have my fist up your balls.
jokes.setup: Threatening someone in a paintball context
jokes.punchline: Mangled tough-guy threat that makes no anatomical sense
jokes.explanation: Absurdist malapropism that's quotable and unexpected - good example of character-specific stupidity

jokes.text: Christina 'Reach-y', I said kick.
jokes.setup: Annie giving commands about not reaching for guns
jokes.punchline: Instant nickname creation based on someone's single action
jokes.explanation: Shows Annie's unexpected tactical side while being efficiently funny - good character work

jokes.text: This is paintball.
jokes.setup: Someone pleading that they played D&D together as friends
jokes.punchline: Annie's cold dismissal treating paintball as more serious than friendship
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character moment - Annie's unexpected ruthlessness delivered with chilling simplicity

jokes.text: Free ice cream and that one guy having a heart attack aren't the only surprises today.
jokes.setup: Dean making announcements about the school picnic
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning someone's medical emergency as a picnic feature
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor showing the Dean's oblivious administrative mindset

jokes.text: Last year's big mistake was way too valuable a prize. And I've been assured-- Eh? That this year will be different.
jokes.setup: Dean promising this year's paintball prize will be more reasonable
jokes.punchline: The hesitation and uncertainty undermining his confidence
jokes.explanation: Sets up expectation that will be immediately subverted - functional setup work

jokes.setup: Promise that this year's prize would be more reasonable
jokes.punchline: Reveals an absurdly large cash prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic escalation - the Dean's assurances immediately proven wrong in spectacular fashion

jokes.text: I heard a rumor you never shoot an unarmed man. Whoever started that rumor didn't eat my beans.
jokes.setup: Western movie trope about not shooting unarmed men
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur about beans as if that explains moral flexibility
jokes.explanation: Absurdist western parody logic - the bean reference is wonderfully random and quotable

jokes.text: Then I guess it's lucky for me I'm not unarmed. Seeing as I'm... Eating your beans.
jokes.setup: Previous exchange about beans and shooting
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're literally eating the other person's beans
jokes.explanation: Good callback that makes the absurd bean logic even more ridiculous by being literal

jokes.text: Yeah? And I want pants. A lot of people want a lot of things.
jokes.setup: Someone saying Jeff wants to see Chang
jokes.punchline: Chang's matter-of-fact response about wanting pants, treating it as equally valid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chang moment - his specific brand of philosophical absurdity delivered with deadpan authority

jokes.text: Math club! Yeah. I'm Asian. You guys Asian? That's pretty racist, man. That wasn't a no. I'm coming over!
jokes.setup: Chang trying to join the math club during paintball
jokes.punchline: His racist assumption followed by doubling down when called out
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang - making racist assumptions then turning criticism into opportunity. The escalation is perfect

jokes.text: Don't shoot me, shoot him. He's white, and he's out of ammo.
jokes.setup: Chang has just joined the math club
jokes.punchline: Immediately betrays his new allies with racial profiling
jokes.explanation: Shows Chang's opportunistic nature and continues the racial profiling theme

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm much safer with you guys.
jokes.setup: Chang just got shot immediately after joining Jeff's group
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about safety while presumably covered in paint
jokes.explanation: Standard sarcastic response but fits Chang's character and situation

jokes.text: Well, I hear he has more paint than a French kindergarten.
jokes.setup: Discussing Pierce's ammunition supplies
jokes.punchline: Specific cultural reference comparing paint supplies to French art education
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that works on multiple levels - both paint as art supplies and ammunition

jokes.text: He's a jerk because we exclude him. We exclude him because he's a jerk.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Pierce's behavior
jokes.punchline: Circular logic that reveals the group's dysfunctional dynamic
jokes.explanation: Perfectly constructed circular logic that exposes character dynamics with great economy

jokes.text: Who wears spurs in paintball?
jokes.setup: Mysterious cowboy character appears in paintball game
jokes.punchline: Questioning the impractical but thematically appropriate footwear choice
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about the absurdity of western cosplay in modern context

jokes.text: He's really good-looking. Like, network TV good-looking. And did you see how big his guns were? Why don't you just marry him, Abed?
jokes.setup: Abed commenting on the mysterious cowboy's appearance
jokes.punchline: Meta-reference to TV production values and Troy's jealous response
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed meta-commentary on TV tropes with Troy's perfectly in-character jealous response

jokes.text: By the power invested in us as deputies, we now pronounce you arrested. Deputies? What are you, Deputies of the Stairwell?
jokes.setup: Troy and Abed announcing they're deputies with authority
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive response questioning their jurisdiction
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that deflates their authority while being specific to the location

jokes.text: My forehead's not that big, right? - Mm. - It's not small.
jokes.setup: Troy asking for reassurance about his forehead size
jokes.punchline: Abed's non-committal and then unhelpful honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Troy's insecurity and Abed's blunt honesty

jokes.text: He's created a safe zone where people can rest, eat, and if necessary, pump their breast milk.
jokes.setup: Describing Pierce's humanitarian efforts in the fort
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly specific and intimate accommodation
jokes.explanation: Unexpected escalation that makes Pierce's fort both more ridiculous and oddly progressive

jokes.text: Are you talking about the handsome blond cowboy with the big guns? Is that guy a student? Who--who is he? We just call him the Black Rider. Okay, but he's not really riding anything.
jokes.setup: Discussing the mysterious cowboy antagonist
jokes.punchline: Annie's literal-minded objection to the nickname
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Annie's need for accuracy even in dramatic situations

jokes.text: I found that people were willing to roll bullets under the door, just for the right to take a dump.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining how his empire started in the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Revealing people's desperate need for basic bathroom access
jokes.explanation: Great insight into both Pierce's opportunism and the basic human needs that create power structures

jokes.text: By the way, for you guys, taking a dump's on the house.
jokes.setup: Just explained how bathroom access became currency
jokes.punchline: Graciously offers free bathroom privileges to his friends
jokes.explanation: Shows Pierce's mix of generosity and power-tripping with efficient callback

jokes.text: Because you're gay.
jokes.setup: Pierce saying he has entertainment more Jeff's speed
jokes.punchline: Pierce's matter-of-fact assumption about Jeff's sexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - casual inappropriate assumption delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: She's a dance major, Jeff. And she loves twinkies.
jokes.setup: Jeff criticizing Pierce making Vicky dance for twinkies
jokes.punchline: Pierce's earnest defense of the arrangement
jokes.explanation: Pierce's oblivious justification shows his warped logic perfectly

jokes.text: For you guys, it means pool parties, dinners, one-night stands. For me... It means counting down the days 'til next fall.
jokes.setup: Pierce talking about what summer means to everyone
jokes.punchline: Revealing his social isolation and dependence on school
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Pierce's tragic loneliness beneath his bravado - genuinely moving and funny

jokes.text: You talkin' about peeing, right? - No. - Me, neither.
jokes.setup: Women mentioning they need to 'pump'
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent misunderstanding and then awkward realization
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Troy's innocence and creates awkward comedy from biological reality

jokes.text: Benjamin Chang, for your betrayal of the Cheerleaders' Alliance, I sentence you to pretendsies death.
jokes.setup: Formal trial proceeding against Chang
jokes.punchline: The made-up word 'pretendsies' undermining the serious tone
jokes.explanation: Creative made-up word that captures the childish nature of the game while maintaining drama

jokes.text: I'm sorry I told Trish about Amber!
jokes.setup: Chang about to be executed by cheerleaders
jokes.punchline: Desperate confession of cheerleader gossip betrayal
jokes.explanation: Shows Chang's integration into cheerleader drama and his desperation in crisis

jokes.text: Stop trying to fluster me with your handsomeness. You're creepy. Then why is your chest getting all flushed? It's a rash. I'm allergic to beans.
jokes.setup: Western standoff flirting between Annie and the Black Rider
jokes.punchline: Annie's awkward excuse connecting back to earlier bean references
jokes.explanation: Good character work for Annie plus unexpected callback to running bean gag

jokes.text: Sorry, I get paid to shoot paintballs, honey. Not the breeze.
jokes.setup: Annie asking if he goes to school there
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's a professional paintball player, not a student
jokes.explanation: Reveals plot information while maintaining his cool cowboy persona

jokes.text: You're dry, sugar.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to shoot with empty gun
jokes.punchline: Western slang for empty gun delivered with condescending endearment
jokes.explanation: Solid Western pastiche with good economy and character voice

jokes.text: I got a better look at him. He's not that good-looking. - Didn't he shoot you? - He tried. He's a bad shot, too? Man, what does this guy have going for him?
jokes.setup: Annie reporting back after encounter with Black Rider
jokes.punchline: Systematic deflation of the mysterious antagonist's appeal
jokes.explanation: Great escalating deflation that builds to Jeff's perfect confused conclusion

jokes.text: Man, what does this guy have going for him?
jokes.setup: Learning Black Rider isn't good-looking or good at shooting
jokes.punchline: Jeff's bewilderment at the guy's lack of apparent skills
jokes.explanation: Good Jeff line showing his competitive nature and confusion

jokes.text: Pierce loaded your gun with blanks.
jokes.setup: Jeff wondering why Annie survived the encounter
jokes.punchline: Reveals Pierce's betrayal and Annie's realization
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that recontextualizes earlier scenes and advances plot with great economy

jokes.text: The key's in my shorts.
jokes.setup: Dean needing to unlock paintball supplies
jokes.punchline: Awkward intimate retrieval situation
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward situation but fits the Dean's character and creates visual comedy

jokes.text: Okay, Black Rider. Now let's see who's attractive.
jokes.setup: Jeff getting properly armed for confrontation
jokes.punchline: Framing the conflict in terms of attractiveness rather than skill
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jeff line - turning everything into a competition about appearance

jokes.text: Dude, you have a problem.
jokes.setup: Jeff's attraction-focused threat to Black Rider
jokes.punchline: Troy's straightforward assessment of Jeff's priorities
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that highlights Jeff's narcissism

jokes.text: I can't go back out there. This is the only life I know.
jokes.setup: Troy found hiding in Pierce's destroyed fort
jokes.punchline: Existential crisis about paintball fort life
jokes.explanation: Shows Troy's tendency to get completely absorbed in role-playing scenarios

jokes.text: So once again, I'm the bad guy. Yes. Well, he got to win last year.
jokes.setup: Pierce confronted about loading guns with blanks
jokes.punchline: His petty justification referencing last season
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Pierce's petty grudge-holding and victim mentality

jokes.text: That's this group's favorite thing. We're always nice to you, Pierce. Wrong. Wrong! Three days ago, I walked in on you guys playing cards without me.
jokes.setup: Annie defending the group's treatment of Pierce
jokes.punchline: Pierce's specific grievance about being excluded from cards
jokes.explanation: Shows Pierce's paranoia and need for inclusion, building to bigger reveal

jokes.text: We weren't playing cards. We were voting, and you weren't invited because we were voting about you. We were taking a vote... On whether we'd invite you back to the group next year.
jokes.setup: Pierce's complaint about being excluded from card game
jokes.punchline: Reveals they were actually voting on his membership
jokes.explanation: Devastating escalation that makes Pierce's paranoia justified and creates genuine pathos

jokes.text: Lucky for you, it had to be unanimous. There was one holdout. One red card.
jokes.setup: Just revealed they voted on Pierce's membership
jokes.punchline: Reveals one person voted to keep him, with visual reference to voting mechanism
jokes.explanation: Good structure creating hope within the devastating reveal

jokes.text: You think you're good-looking, but you're not. You're average. You're just an average-looking guy with a big chin.
jokes.setup: Black Rider confronting Jeff
jokes.punchline: Attacking Jeff's core insecurity about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect attack on Jeff's vanity with specific physical details that land hard

jokes.text: Last week he did it to get out of letting me have a stick of gum.
jokes.setup: Pierce apparently having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: Jeff's oddly specific example of Pierce's fake medical emergency history
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd specificity showing Pierce's petty manipulation and Jeff's experience with it

jokes.text: Sucker! Told you, dude. I'm the best! You're the worst.
jokes.setup: Pierce's fake heart attack working
jokes.punchline: Gleeful revelation and immediate trash talk
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce moment showing his petty victory celebration

jokes.text: I wouldn't go to this toilet with my big sister's toe.
jokes.setup: Black Rider explaining why he doesn't go to school here
jokes.punchline: Bizarre anatomical insult construction
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd insult construction that's memorable for its weirdness

jokes.text: I got Coldplay tickets.
jokes.setup: Black Rider abandoning his mission
jokes.punchline: Mundane reason for leaving epic paintball battle
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the mysterious antagonist with mundane concert priorities

jokes.text: I was paid to be here and win the game so my boss could keep his prize money. You work for the ice cream company? Sweetie, this thing is so much bigger than you can even imagine.
jokes.setup: Black Rider revealing he's not a student
jokes.punchline: Hints at larger conspiracy beyond simple ice cream sponsorship
jokes.explanation: Good plot advancement with ominous implications for continuation

jokes.text: I got Coldplay tickets.
jokes.setup: Black Rider explaining he's leaving this important conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Reveals mundane concert plans as his real priority
jokes.explanation: Perfect bathos deflating all dramatic tension with relatable mundane priority

jokes.text: Resistance is as pointless as your degrees.
jokes.setup: Military-style invasion announcement to community college
jokes.punchline: Insulting both their resistance and their education quality
jokes.explanation: Sharp dig at community college education wrapped in military intimidation

jokes.text: - Are we the little rascals? - I was.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if they're like the Little Rascals gang
jokes.punchline: Pierce's matter-of-fact claim to have been an actual Little Rascal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce line combining his age with his tendency for grandiose claims

jokes.text: Once I'm gone, I'm gone. Then I guess I'll see you in hell.
jokes.setup: Final dramatic moment before invasion
jokes.punchline: Jeff's matter-of-fact acceptance of damnation
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with Jeff's characteristic cynicism

jokes.text: Once I'm gone, I'm gone. Then I guess I'll see you in hell. That's just the tip of the iceberg.
jokes.setup: Dramatic final confrontation dialogue between Pierce and Jeff
jokes.punchline: Pierce's mixed metaphor combining hell and icebergs
jokes.explanation: Great malapropism that shows Pierce trying to be dramatic but failing with mixed metaphors

tmdb_overview: A $100,000 paintball war prize pits members of the study group against one another, until they decide to join forces to stop a mysterious challenger.
insight: Western pastiche sustains 55 jokes across 23 minutes but underdelivers on impact.
